_comment: Single source of truth for the TBox power tree. tools/gen_power_tree.py regenerates the schematic tree graphic and per-stage load annotations in kicad/supply.kicad_sch from this file. Do not hand-edit the generated block in the schematic. Per-node max_ma/max_basis is the supply's deliverable maximum (rated current, or the thermal limit where that binds first); _max_analysis records the derivation and is not drawn. alert_utilization is the fraction of max_ma at which tools/check_power.py (TODO) should alert.

input.name: 13V8_IN
input.note: PTC 0.5A hold vs ~210 mA total draw

nodes.name: RAW_13V8
nodes._max_analysis: No conversion stage; capability is the input PTC hold current, shared with everything downstream (incl. LM2940 input current).

nodes.name: +9V
nodes._max_analysis: Rated 1A but package-Pd-limited: Tj 125C, Ta 50C, thetaJA ~50C/W (TO-263 on copper pour, VERIFY against layout) -> Pd 1.5W; at 15V in (6V drop) I <= 250 mA. At 13.8V in (4.8V drop) ~310 mA.
nodes.sch_note:
load 130 mA typ / 180 mA max
Pd <=1W @15V; dropout OK to 10V5

nodes.name: BIAS_5V
nodes._max_analysis: Rated 100 mA; thermal is comparable at 9V in (4V drop, TO-92 thetaJA ~180C/W, Ta 50C -> ~100 mA), so the rating binds.

nodes.name: VREF
nodes._max_analysis: NE5532 output: ~38 mA short-circuit typ; ~10 mA is a sane continuous drive limit for an AC ground reference.
